summary: colorpicker-ui postinstall harvests installer secrets and stages code injection into adjacent frontend workspace
details: The package advertises itself as a minimal React color picker but its postinstall hook (`node setup.js`, declared in package.json) performs aggressive host reconnaissance and credential theft on every `npm install`. setup.js shells out via child_process to read `/etc/passwd`, the Kubernetes service-account token at `/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token` (and its namespace file), and queries the cloud instance metadata service at `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/`, while also dumping the full `process.env` and listing `/` and `/app/`. The harvested data is serialized as a TypeScript module (`export default {...}`) and written to `/app/sandbox-data/workspace/frontend/_rce.ts` and `<cwd>/../frontend/_rce.ts`, planting attacker-controlled content into a sibling frontend project so that the next `npm run build` will compile and ship the stolen secrets (and any code embedded in them) inside the victim's application bundle. A console message `colorpicker-ui: generating default theme cache...` is printed as cover for the recon. None of this behavior is required by, or related to, the advertised color-picker functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
